executive_summary.verdict: Mixed — needs more data, but competition-heavy
executive_summary.why_it_matters: Strong urgent care, cna training demand near the anchor.
executive_summary.biggest_risk: No commercial storefront anchor was identified — the nearest hit was "Doc's Office". Treat this candidate as a coordinate to validate with a commercial broker, not as a confirmed business location.
executive_summary.best_strategy: Target nursing-student certification, weekend fast-certification classes, EMS/fire workforce training.
executive_summary.before_leasing: Validate parking and site access, real class demand (calls / site visits), the missing data fields listed per candidate.
next_actions: Validate rent and parking near Doc's Office.
Contact nearby healthcare schools and employers to gauge partnership interest.
Run a small paid-search / landing-page test before signing a lease.
executive_summary.long_summary: The executive verdict recommends the San Jose — sixth street corridor as the best area-level candidate, with a score of 56.4/100. This area is promising but needs more data and field validation due to a lack of a commercial storefront anchor. The strongest advantage is the training ecosystem, with a score of 83/100. The best strategy is to target nursing-student certification, weekend fast-certification classes, and EMS/fire workforce training. The confidence level is very high, at 85/100. Before leasing, it is recommended to validate parking and site access, real class demand, and missing data fields. The area has strong demand for urgent care and CNA training, with AHA BLS being the strongest course type, averaging 9.9 students per class.
executive_summary.data_confidence_note: Based on 2632 historical classes across 128 ZIP codes. Scores are decision-support, not guaranteed enrollment predictions.
